title: The Frisco Bar
location: The Frisco Bar & Grill, 6750 Gaylord Pkwy ste.120, Frisco, TX 75034, USA
description:
Can arrive as early as 7:00 to get set up and do a quick sound check. 

Ask for Adam (Brittany, who booked us, will not be there that night)

Show starts at 9:00pm.  Show ends promptly at 1:00pm.  

They have in-house PA System, we will only bring our instruments, monitors, and a mixer to plug into the system.  

The system will be powered up and ready to go or that someone will be there who knows how to turn it on.

We will not mic drums through to the PA system (we have a mic on one drum that only plays on in-ear monitors) but absolutely  no sound amplified to the audience to help keep the noise under control.

title: Jackson Crossing @ Watters Creek
location: Watters Creek at Montgomery Farm, 970 Garden Park Dr, Allen, TX 75013, USA
description:
Location: Watters Creek at Montgomery Farm

US 75 & Bethany Road
Allen, TX 75013
Performance Fee: $500
Fee is payable upon performance. Fee will be paid via company check.

Attire is to be: Upscale casual

Lighting will be provided via one (1) tripod light.

Sound board will not be provided. 

Food or Drink will not be provided—Artist may bring bottled water.

Please note we are a non-smoking property with a zero tolerance. We do not allow outside food on our green. 

Please keep all lyrics family friendly.

Artist to load in and complete any sound check required a minimum of one (1) hour prior to performance.

Performance to start promptly at the above indicated time.

Artist will take two (2) ten minute breaks between extended sets and will come prepared to provide background music during break via, iPod.

SPECIAL INSTRUCTIONS: Watters Creek is an outdoor venue. The management of Watters Creek reserves the right to cancel this agreement due to inclement weather up to noon the day of the performance without monetary penalty. Once on site and if inclement weather is reasonable threat, Artist has the authority to judge unsafe performance condition. Upon such notification, the management of Watters Creek must move the performance indoors or forfeit this performance and full payment of the performance fee.

The management of Watters Creek reserves the right to terminate this agreement with appropriate notice up to (7) seven days prior to performance without penalty.

ARTIST IS RESPONSIBLE FOR PROVIDING ALL BACKLINE, I.E., PERCUSSION, GUITAR AND/OR KEYBOARD AMPS AND ANY OTHER EQUIPMENT OR MUSICAL INSTRUMENTS NEEDED FOR THE PERFORMANCE.

title: Gilbert Velasquez 50th Birthday Party
location: 245 Crepe Myrtle Ln, Murphy, TX 75094, USA
description:
Booked through TheBash.com.  Net $475.

Backyard concert. Buck & Ray doing walkthrough on 7/28.


We can arrive as early as 6pm (Kyle)

Sound check at 7:30

Play 8-10 with optional 5 minute break (leave recorded music playing)

Client is letting us build the set list.  Client recognizes this is very "last minute" and seems very flexible as a result.

Notes from visit:


Buck & I visited with Mr. Velasquez today.  His yard is small and his neighborhood is dense - so the biggest concerns are space and noise control.  Anything we can do to keep the noise controllable (Don, Kyle) should help.

Buck suggested not bringing the subs and making sure the PA's are pointed toward the house instead of the neighborhood to help.

The host was very accommodating, but a little stressed over how quickly everything is being pulled together.  He has electricians  upgrading some circuits tomorrow to support 2 large swamp coolers (one pointed at us!).  Buck believes our power requirements won't be a problem.

The host said he will clear the area Buck and I picked.  We can make game-time changes if needed, but hopefully won't need to since the swamp coolers will be in place and furniture will have been moved.

The map below shows the driveway which the owner will block off for us when we arrive. He has a metal bar that can prop the gate of the privacy fence open. There's a rough path from driveway to paved patio marked with a yellow line.  

(Map in email)



His wife contributed some song ideas -  fortunately, her list included anything by Journey, the Eagles, or Chicago.  We shared our bandmix list with him and he said it all looked good.  (For future reference, anything "Chris Stapleton" was on the list too!)


The host was very accommodating - even asked what kind of beer we like - they will have food and drinks and we're welcome to enjoy.  I asked about coming in shorts and t-shirts, he said "of course".  He promised no splashing in the pool.

I would like to show up at 6 along with Kyle - get set up - then go inside or somewhere nearby to cool off before show time.  As long as everyone is ready by 7:30 for a final sound check - then we can chill til 8.

While we're loading everything back up - we may have to nudge a few guests since the pathway in and out is narrow.

title: Don Going to see Primus in Irving
The Maverick
description:
Our contact is Abby Tracey.

$500


The stage is right by the front door - gotta load in through the main entrance. It's  roughly a right-triangle - It's about 17 feet across the front - each of the sides go back 13 feet.  Bigger than Frisco and better laid out.

We have to bring our own sound.  All their plugs are on the left side of stage (from audience view) but there were a lot of them

They have stage lights - but some more would be better.  

My rough guess is that they can seat about 200-250 people - with standing room back by games and pool tables.


They had a little place by the door to store excess equipment - they said no one ever sits there.

title: Powder Creek Pavillion
location: 520 S Center St, Bonham, TX 75418, USA
description:
$500 intro rate, $150 Travel, $650 Total
The facility itself is on over 5 acres the majority commonly used for parking. The pavilion roof over the entertainment area is 92 ft. long X 86 ft. wide X 20 ft. tall. We have tables and chairs for 400 People with plenty of room for lawn chairs and such. We provide a 32 ft. X 24 ft. X 4 ft. stage area with stairs and ramp. We provide large barrel fans for circulation, restroom facilities, and a concession stand. For personal events catering is permitted. Any Alcoholic beverages consumed must be in compliance with Texas State Law. 
The sound system is a 40,000 W digital sound system 12 X 18" Sub-woofers, 10 mid/hi arrays, up to 6 monitor mixes. all the mics and cables required. Professional sound engineer operating the system. 
Computerized LED DMX Lighting, 50 LED Pars, 6 ~ 230W 7R Sharpies Moving Heads, a dozen smaller moving heads, dance floor effect lighting, and hazer. 
​ $10 cover.  Coolers allowed 

 Load in at 5

Contact: Rick Massey

title: Birthday Party - Confirmed
description:
Our contact:  Chelsea Mathews (daughter)  410 688 8594 

The former mayor of Waxahachie, Mr. Kevin Strength is turning 60.  

His family is throwing him a huge party at his party-friendly ranch house on Lake Waxahachie.  They are expecting 150-200 guests since he is very well known (former fire fighter, lots of friends and family)  Venue is described as huge ranch house on the lake.  The entire town is invited, so no worries about noise complaints from neighbors.  Check out the map & street view here:  https://goo.gl/maps/NdyMuwLSQKRyNkFU6

The seem well-organized (despite last minute band hiring!) - covered, dance floor, maybe a stage, portable air conditioned outdoor toilets (since the ranch is on septic) - they seem to be very much on their game!

Typical party stuff - we pick the set list.  Her whole family loves music - many play guitar.   Guest of honor has a guitar collection. They may use our sound system for toasts, roasts, etc.  Very casual & flexible.

She said there's beer, wine, drinks, BBQ and more - we're welcome to it.  Inside rooms and private bathrooms for us if needed. 

Timeline communicated to client:

  • Hosts arrive early, guests start to arrive at 2:30
  • Drummer (Kyle) should arrive first - probably between 2-2:30 - he will ask for you - figure out where we play, power, everything we need to know  - up front so we can get out of your way and let you enjoy the party too!
  • Other band members will arrive over next 90 minutes.
  • Sound check about 3:30 (approximate)
  • Downbeat 4:00 (unless host wants it any later)
  • We plan 3 short breaks with music running - but any time you need to use the sound system to talk to guests or some quiet - just let us know.
  • We will wrap up at 8:00pm.  Usually takes about 30 minutes to tear down and load up.
  • We can take final payment of $850 balance by check, cash, Venmo, or PayPal. 
  • We aim for 5-star reviews on TheBash - so please let us know if anything else is needed.
Payment Details:


$850 Band fee
$150 Travel
$1000 Total
$100 already paid via TheBash
$900 Balance payable by cash, check, Venmo or PayPal

title: Don Playing with Red Line Drift
Sue & Stu Out with Marianne and Dann
description:
Wondering who might be interested in an evening at Pete’s dueling pianos at the star on Friday evening, June 30? I can reserve a table that would run approximately $20 per person for a group of 10 -12 depending on who is interested. We would want to arrive at about 7 PM and I’m thinking stay through approximately 10:30 PM to 11 PM? Dueling pianos is a ton of fun! we could always reserve a second table if there were enough interested here, and I could invite others.
Thanking that they would probably not want to split checks, I think we would want to be prepared to handle drinks and food with a single check at the end and I would be happy to settle up and accept cash later.

title: Risky Business at The Village Dallas
location: The Village Dallas, 5605 Village Glen Dr, Dallas, TX 75206, USA
description:
Day of show instructions

We supply sound.

Parking
Parking is available free of charge on The Village property. The leasing office will advise on the best parking location to make loading in as efficient as possible.

Load In/Out
Upon arrival to The Village, please check in at the leasing office. Someone from the leasing office will provide you with directions to the Event Lawn.

re: sound, you all will have to provide full sound, since the venue's sound is only for smaller shows

re: DOS contact -- Jennifer Pena at 469-235-6994 is your first line of contact

re: merchandising and t-shirt raffling, unfortunately neither will be possible

accepting tips is ok


Setup
The Village will not be providing any sound/backline equipment for the Event Lawn performances. As such, bands must bring all inputs necessary for a successful performance (PA, mics, stands, cables, instruments, etc.).



Run of Show Preparation times 
4:45 PM Load-in 
6:00 PM Doors
Set times
6:00 PM-9:00 PM
Food brought to us in 2nd break 


General Instructions
The Village Dallas welcomes you to perform at The Village Country Club! Upon arrival to The Village, please check in at the Sales Office, as they will direct you to the Country Club.   The Village is looking for artists to perform a set fully composed of upbeat covers. Please plan your set accordingly, and avoid originals/sad/slow songs.   The Village Country Club will provide artists with a PA System (2 Mains) and XLR cables -- mics and mic stands can be made available upon request.   Singer-Songwriters should plan to bring their own Mics, Stands, Instrument Cables, as well as any other inputs necessary for a successful performance. 

All Artists - PLEASE BRING A BACKUP XLR CABLE in case of any technical difficulties.   Artists will be provided with a $25 tab for FOOD ONLY -- consuming alcohol before or during your set is prohibited.

title: Gwen Bhela

title: Charlie's Lakeside at Pier 121
location: Charlie's Lakeside Bar and Grill, 1481 E Hill Park Rd, Lewisville, TX 75056, USA
description:
$500
Contact - Jason Norman 972-832-7911
We supply sound/lights
Tips/merch allowed

Jackson Crossing Band,
This is confirmation that Jackson Crossing Band will be playing at Charlie's Lakeside Bar and Grill on the night of Saturday July 20th, 2024. The Band will play from 8-11pm. Per Lewisville city ordinance the music must conclude in full by 11pm.
Compensation for this night will be $500 and paid by check at the conclusion of the performance.
Please note that in the event of inclement weather on the day of the show we will communicate with the point of contact at the earliest possible time to determine if cancellation is necessary. If cancellation is necessary then compensation will not be paid. As a courtesy we will do everything we can to rebook the event at a later date.
All band equipment must be provided by the band. The establishment is not required and may not be able to provide any equipment such as extension cords, batteries, lights, stools etc.
The band stage is equipped with multiple electrical outlets for equipment use.
The band is allowed to hang banners or other merchandise materials, but may not cover any Charlies/ establishment logos on the stage. Any banners or merchandise that cover establishment logos will be required to move or must be taken down.
If the band chooses to sell merchandise they must provide their own equipment to sell, and must be able to handle all sales transitions without the use of Charlie's staff members.
Please review this confirmation letter, and if agreed to, kindly confirm these terms and conditions. We are looking forward to Jackson Crossing play at Charlie's Lakeside Bar and Grill.
